target.title: fix: hydrate current turn image attachments
target.body:
## Root cause
Current inbound images from Feishu/IM can arrive with structured `MediaPaths` but without reliable `image/*` MIME metadata. After #76659 limited prompt image scanning to the visible prompt, those images should be passed through the current-turn media path instead of being rediscovered by scanning reply/runtime context text.

## Fix
- Keep `detectAndLoadPromptImages` scoped to the visible prompt; this does not restore runtime/reply context scanning.
- In `current-turn-images.ts`, infer an image MIME from the current-turn `MediaPath`/`MediaPaths` extension only when the declared media type is missing or generic binary.
- Preserve explicit non-image MIME values such as `application/pdf`, `audio/*`, or `video/*`; those are not upgraded to images based on extension.
- Pass the narrowed, current-turn-only MIME hint into the existing `resolveAgentTurnAttachments` path; `agent-turn-attachments.ts` stays unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu/IM-style current-turn image attachments with `MediaPaths` but no `MediaTypes` are hydrated as native image input instead of relying on prompt/runtime-context path scanning.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b338d73c97`, macOS, Node.js v22.22.2, pnpm/tsx from the repository install.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary 1x1 PNG file, then invoked `resolveCurrentTurnImages` from `src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.ts` with a Feishu-like context containing `MediaPaths: [imagePath]`, `MediaWorkspaceDir`, and no `MediaTypes`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout):

```json
{
  "images": 1,
  "mimeType": "image/png",
  "imageOrder": [
    "inline"
  ],
  "base64Length": 92
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The current-turn attachment path produced one native image input with MIME `image/png` and inline image order, proving the attachment no longer depends on scanning reply/runtime context text.
- What was not tested: A live Feishu production message was not sent from the real Feishu bot; this proof exercises the OpenClaw current-turn media hydration path with the same structured fields that Feishu/IM provide.

## Verification
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts`
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts --testNamePattern "hydrates current image MediaPaths by extension" --reporter=verbose`
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts --testNamePattern "does not fall back to recent history images when the current turn has non-image media" --reporter=verbose`
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- Direct module check: `MediaTypes: ["application/pdf"]` with a `.png` path returns `{ "images": 0, "imageOrder": [] }`.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): stage sandboxed workspace media
target.body:
Fixes #74061.

## Summary

- Stages absolute media paths that are already inside the agent workspace before trying sandbox path translation.
- Keeps absolute host-local paths outside the workspace blocked when sandbox mapping fails.
- Allows host-read HTML documents only through the same byte-level text validation path used for CSV and Markdown, with binary-disguised HTML rejection coverage.
- Adds an explicit WhatsApp QA scenario return type so the existing RTT proof object keeps its literal `request-to-observed-message` source type and production typecheck stays green on current `main`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram/local final-reply `MEDIA:` paths that point to files inside the agent workspace can be dropped when a sandbox workspace exists because sandbox path translation runs before the normal local staging path. The reported repro includes `/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/.../screenshot.png` and a latest-build `.html` workspace report receiving only `Media failed`.

- Real environment tested: Current OpenClaw `upstream/main` worktree at `8da8bc4aad` on Ubuntu 24.04 with the repository dependency set installed by `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the patched worktree, ran:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the patched OpenClaw worktree:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.media.config.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 7.27s
```

Additional local type validation:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-86531-core.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-86531-extensions-2.tsbuildinfo
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:canary
```

- Observed result after fix: The reply media normalizer now stages `/Users/peter/.openclaw/workspace/reports/report.html` to `/tmp/outbound-media/report.html` even when `ensureSandboxWorkspaceForSession()` returns a sandbox workspace, instead of dropping the path before delivery. The outside-workspace host path case remains blocked. Valid host-read HTML loads as `text/html`, while opaque binary, binary tails, UTF-16 BOM garbage, NUL-padded bytes, and high-byte disguised `.html` files are rejected.

- What was not tested: I did not send a live Telegram Bot API message from the reporter's environment because those channel credentials are not available in this checkout. The proof exercises the production normalizer/host-read code path in the repository test harness with the reported absolute workspace `.html` shape.

target.title: fix(gateway): disable provider auth prewarm by default
target.body:
What does this PR do?
- Stops the gateway startup path from enabling provider-auth prewarming unless it is explicitly opted in.
- Adds a regression test that verifies the startup path does not schedule the prewarm sidecar by default.

Root cause
- The post-attach startup flow treated `providerAuthPrewarm` as enabled unless callers explicitly set `enabled: false`, so the normal gateway boot path always scheduled the prewarm work.

Fix
- Require `providerAuthPrewarm.enabled === true` before scheduling provider-auth prewarming.
- Add a regression test that passes the startup config without `enabled: true` and verifies no prewarm sidecar is scheduled.

Why this shape
- Keeps the prewarm hook available for explicit opt-in flows.
- Makes the default gateway boot path lighter and avoids startup CPU spikes.

Tests
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`

Related PRs / issues
- Fixes #86512

target.title: fix(codex): retire MCP app-server helpers
target.body:
## Summary
- Add shared Codex app-server client leases so active runs keep a shared app-server alive.
- Mark stdio app-server clients for retire-on-idle after turns that projected user MCP servers, closing the app-server process group only after active leases release.
- Cover retire-on-idle behavior in shared-client tests and preserve existing thread unsubscribe cleanup.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-thread-cleanup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: Completed Codex app-server turns that start projected user MCP stdio servers no longer leave the shared app-server process alive indefinitely just to preserve client reuse.

Real environment tested: Ash macOS OpenClaw workspace with the live Codex app-server and Linear MCP configuration from TRR-25.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node integrations/linear-openclaw-bridge/scripts/check-helper-processes.mjs --owner-uid $(id -u) --min-age-seconds 180`, targeted SIGTERM of the reported Ash-owned stale Linear MCP process groups, then `mcporter call linear-stdio.save_comment ...`.

Evidence after fix: Live terminal output from the verifier reported 18 stale Ash-owned Linear MCP helper groups before cleanup, with ages between about 300 and 1047 seconds. After targeted SIGTERM, `ps -u ash` returned no `mcp-remote https://mcp.linear.app/mcp` rows, and `mcporter call linear-stdio.save_comment ...` returned a successful Linear comment JSON payload for TRR-25 comment `b6511717-1291-4a65-b402-d0ff03c1fe23`.

Observed result after fix: The stale Ash-owned Linear MCP helpers were gone, the built-in stale transport failure was reproduced, and a fresh Linear MCP stdio connection successfully posted to TRR-25. The code now retires MCP-backed stdio shared app-server clients after active leases drain, which is the lifecycle point that owns those child MCP helper process groups.

What was not tested: I did not restart a production installed OpenClaw runtime from this branch under concurrent real user traffic; concurrent active-client safety is covered by the shared-client lease tests.

Linear: https://linear.app/picardandelaine/issue/TRR-25/fix-codex-linear-mcp-helper-lifecycle-leaks

target.title: fix(gateway): skip transcript resolution for missing files
target.body:
What does this PR do?
- Avoids resolving a session key from a transcript path when the transcript file no longer exists.
- Adds a regression test that verifies a missing transcript path does not reach the broadcast path.

Root cause
- The transcript broadcast path still attempted to resolve a session key from stale transcript metadata even when the underlying transcript file was already gone.

Fix
- Guard transcript-key resolution with `fs.existsSync(...)` before looking up the session key.
- Add a test that calls the broadcast handler with a missing transcript file and asserts no resolution or broadcast happens.

Why this shape
- Preserves explicit `sessionKey` updates.
- Only changes the fallback path that can resurrect stale transient sessions.

Tests
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-session-events.test.ts`

Related PRs / issues

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: rebased PR #86210 onto current upstream main and re-proved the multi-slot memory contract from the refreshed head. This covers canonical memory role slots (`memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, `memory.userModel`), legacy `plugins.slots.memory` compatibility, memory-only per-agent slot overrides, plugin-loader/runtime dispatch, LosslessClaw as global `contextEngine`, and self-hosted Honcho memory integration.
- Real environment tested: Dev-01 local checkout at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/workspace/repos/openclaw`, branch `feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`, rebased PR head `aeca11a7cd70f8e6d3e23b5c9a817f38b5dbe37e` on upstream main `aa702cf3dbe7d89bb49752e2dc7ff5be0a69bcfd`. Remote live proof ran on Orion/Nano-01 at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-86210`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, Docker/Compose/Buildx with NVIDIA runtime, `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`, `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.2`, and a local/self-hosted Honcho stack at `127.0.0.1:8000` backed by PostgreSQL/pgvector and Redis.
- Exact steps or command run after fix: fetched current upstream main, rebased the PR branch, resolved conflicts in the Pi embedded extension path without dropping the PR slot-resolution changes, regenerated the config docs baseline, committed `chore(config): refresh schema baseline after rebase`, force-with-lease pushed the rebased fork branch, refreshed Orion to the pushed head, rebuilt, validated config, reran targeted memory/plugin/config/doctor/runtime suites, reran live model and live gateway proof, reran self-hosted Honcho proof, reran LosslessClaw runtime proof, and reran the legacy `v2026.5.20` upgrade proof through PR-head `doctor --fix`.

- What was not tested: hosted `api.honcho.dev` was not used; the Honcho proof is self-hosted/local on Orion. A broad local `pnpm test` pass was attempted but not used as the gate because unrelated environment-sensitive shards failed in this OpenClaw Dev environment; the affected media/image slices were rebuilt and rerun cleanly, and the requested targeted memory/plugin/config/doctor/runtime plus live proof gates passed after the rebase. Future owner-provenance object semantics from #70823 remain future work.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

target.title: fix(cli): preserve Discord voice outbound helper
target.body:
Summary
- Keep CLI lazy outbound dependency routing from treating Discord voice helper keys as normal channel adapters.
- Preserve explicitly supplied `discordVoice` / `sendDiscordVoice` helpers while leaving missing helpers unresolved so the Discord runtime can decide the voice path.
- Add focused regression coverage for the CLI deps proxy and outbound send mapping, plus a changelog fix entry for #84952.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 4 files, 106 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: Discord cron announces with `messages.tts.auto="always"` could fail before the Discord voice-message helper ran because the CLI outbound deps proxy manufactured a fake channel adapter for the `discordVoice` helper lookup.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu source checkout on Node 22 with focused CLI, Discord outbound adapter, and cron double-announce regression tests. Crabbox/live Discord delivery was not run for this source-level fix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: The new CLI deps regression asserts `createDefaultDeps().discordVoice` and `createDefaultDeps().sendDiscordVoice` remain `undefined` instead of becoming unavailable channel adapters, and the outbound mapping regression asserts lazy channel factories are not called for those helper keys while explicit helpers are still preserved.

Observed result after fix: Focused regression suite passed: 4 files, 106 tests passed. Formatting, lint, diff check, and the branch was rebased onto current `main` with an SSH-signed head commit.

What was not tested: A live Discord cron announce with generated TTS audio was not run before opening this PR; the proof is focused source-level regression coverage for the broken dependency lookup path.

Fixes #84952

target.title: fix(auto-reply): warn on stranded message_tool_only final reply (#85714)
target.body:
AI-assisted (Claude Code). 🤖

## Summary

Under `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, OpenClaw keeps the agent's final assistant text **private** and delivers visible output only through explicit `message(action=send)` calls. When the model occasionally omits the delivery tool on its final turn and emits the reply as plain text (instead of the `NO_REPLY` sentinel), that real reply is **silently dropped** — the user sees an unresponsive bot even though the turn completed normally (#85714).

This patch makes the loss **observable** without changing the delivery contract. When a `message_tool_only` turn produces a real final reply but nothing was delivered this turn, the gateway logs a WARN. It deliberately does **not** auto-deliver the text: that would defeat the documented "keep normal final text private" guarantee and risk double-delivery or leaking internal-only content.

The stranded check keys off the **actual assistant final text** (`rawAssistantText`), not the assembled payload bundle — so verbose notices, raw trace, and the usage line never trigger a false warn on an intentional `NO_REPLY` turn.

Refs #85714 (tagged `needs-product-decision` / `needs-maintainer-review`): this proposes the conservative non-silent-signal option, not a delivery-semantics change.

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/reply/stranded-source-reply.ts` (new): `isStrandedMessageToolReply` (pure guard) + `warnStrandedMessageToolReply` (logs `sessionKey` / `channel` / char count only — never the body).
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`: warn at the `message_tool_only` suppression seam, keyed off the assistant final text.
- Unit test + `runReplyAgent` integration cases: WARN fires on a stranded reply; stays silent when the tool delivered; stays silent on a `NO_REPLY` turn with metadata payloads.
- `qa/scenarios/channels/message-tool-stranded-final-reply.md` (new): qa-channel scenario that asserts the `source-reply/stranded` WARN in the live gateway log.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A real final reply under `messages.visibleReplies: message_tool` is silently dropped (nothing reaches the channel) when the model does not call the delivery tool; after this patch the running gateway emits an observable `source-reply/stranded` WARN.

Real environment tested: A real OpenClaw gateway process (QA child gateway + qa-channel transport) driving a **live model over an OpenAI-compatible endpoint** — `openai/deepseek-v4-pro` (DeepSeek). No mock model: the run reports `mock requests=0`. The agent runs the normal `runReplyAgent` delivery pipeline.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw qa suite --scenario message-tool-stranded-final-reply --provider-mode live-frontier --model openai/deepseek-v4-pro`

Evidence after fix: copied live console output from the real gateway run against the live model:

```console
(qa-suite-report.md) Status: pass
Details: no-outbound stranded reply; WARN logged=true; mock requests=0; gateway log:
  2026-05-26T00:07:20.980+08:00 [source-reply/stranded] agent produced a final reply but never called the configured delivery tool (message_tool_only); response kept private and not delivered to the source channel
Runs against qa-channel + qa-lab bus + real gateway child + live frontier models (openai/deepseek-v4-pro)
```

Observed result after fix: under live `openai/deepseek-v4-pro`, the direct turn produced a real final reply, the gateway delivered nothing to the originating conversation (the #85714 stranding reproduced — zero outbound), and the gateway logged the `source-reply/stranded` WARN above. The scenario asserts that WARN, so it fails if the warn path regresses.

What was not tested: a real chat transport (Telegram/WebChat) — the live model ran over the qa-channel synthetic transport, not a production channel. The intentional-`NO_REPLY`-with-metadata no-false-positive path is covered by the `runReplyAgent` integration test rather than a live run.

## Verification (supplemental)

- Unit + integration tests pass (`stranded-source-reply.test.ts`, `agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` / `pnpm tsgo:core:test` exit 0; oxlint 0/0; oxfmt clean; `pnpm build` exit 0.
- QA scenario also green on the deterministic `--provider-mode mock-openai` lane.
- Rebased on latest `upstream/main`.

target.title: fix(updater): exclude prerelease tags from stable git channel
target.body:
## Summary

- classify semver prerelease git tags as non-stable when deriving the effective update channel
- keep beta git tags on the beta channel while routing other prerelease tags to dev
- update the release-channel docs and focused tests for prerelease tag handling

## Verification

- `node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval '...'` passed after the final rebase: direct update-channel prerelease probe passed (8 checks)
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed
- `pnpm docs:list` completed; in this Codex worktree it first ran pnpm dependency reconciliation before listing docs
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed before commit: no accepted/actionable findings reported
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed after the final rebase: no accepted/actionable findings reported
- `timeout 120s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-channels.test.ts --reporter=verbose` timed out locally under Node 26.0.0 with no useful test output, matching the known local Vitest/Testbox hang blocker from the previous run

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Stable git-channel resolution no longer treats semver prerelease tags like `v2026.5.25-alpha.1`, `-next.1`, or numeric prereleases as stable targets; beta tags still resolve to beta.
Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on Node 26.0.0.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval '...'` importing `src/infra/update-channels.ts` and checking beta, stable, and prerelease channel classification.
Evidence after fix: The direct probe printed `update-channel prerelease probe passed (8 checks)`.
Observed result after fix: Alpha, next, and numeric prerelease git tags resolve away from stable while stable and beta tags keep their expected channels.
What was not tested: Targeted Vitest/Testbox proof did not complete locally; the bounded `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-channels.test.ts --reporter=verbose` attempt timed out after 120 seconds under Node 26.0.0, and Node 25 was previously unusable because `libllhttp.so.9.3` was missing.

target.title: Doctor: expose UI freshness health findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes Control UI protocol freshness as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps real build/rebuild mutation on the existing `maybeRepairUiProtocolFreshness()` path used by `openclaw doctor --fix`.
- Keeps broad structured repair in preview-only mode for this rule: dry-run reports process effects, while non-dry-run remains owned by the legacy repair path.
- Hardens the stale detector so a successful empty git history does not create false stale findings, while unavailable git history still reports the mtime mismatch without commit details.
- Skips build/rebuild prompts when the checkout does not contain `ui/` sources.

This PR does not change the public health/plugin SDK contract and does not invent file diffs for generated UI assets.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw doctor --lint` can report missing/stale Control UI assets, and dry-run repair output can preview the build/rebuild process effect without mutating generated UI files or changing the existing `openclaw doctor --fix` repair ownership.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-ui-freshness-stack`, head `f2a6b4af48`, source harness using the real doctor health registry and temporary ignored `dist/control-ui/index.html` states. Paths below are redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git diff --check`

target.title: Add /goal session continuation command
target.body:
## Summary

- add a bundled `goal` extension with `/goal help`, `/goal start`, `/goal status`, `/goal events`, `/goal pause`, `/goal resume`, `/goal done`, and `/goal clear`
- add a `goal_status` tool so the active session can report `continue`, `done`, `blocked`, `paused`, or `waiting_approval`
- store one visible goal state per trusted session
- use the continuation lease API from the scaffold PR to enqueue one same-session follow-up turn
- add stop gates, terminal states, and a continuation cap

## Dependency

Depends on the scaffold PR: #85722.

This PR is stacked on the scaffold branch locally so maintainers can review the runtime contract separately from the user-facing feature. On GitHub it targets `main`; until #85722 merges, the diff may include the scaffold changes as well. I am happy to keep the two PRs stacked, retarget after #85722 lands, or combine them if maintainers prefer one review thread.

## Why

The scaffold PR is deliberately small, but a runtime primitive is hard to judge without a real user flow. `/goal` is that user flow: a human starts an objective, inspects what happened, pauses or resumes continuation, and finishes or clears the goal while the model can request only bounded same-session continuation through `goal_status`.

The user experience is not only "let the agent continue." It is also "let the human see whether the agent is behaving well enough to keep continuing." That matters across model tiers: a frontier model may stay on target most of the time, but cheaper or smaller models need tighter rails, clearer stop states, and a visible decision trail so users can trust, pause, resume, or stop work without guessing.

This is why the command surface includes both lifecycle controls and lightweight observability. `/goal status` shows the current state; `/goal events` shows the recent decision trail. The user does not need a dashboard or debug mode to answer the basic question: "is this still doing the right thing?"

## Safety

- slash-command session context is host-owned
- `goal_status` uses trusted tool context, not model-supplied `sessionKey` or `goalId`
- model-driven `continue` is accepted only while the current goal is already in `continue`
- `done` and `blocked` are terminal for `/goal resume`; start a new goal to continue
- `paused` stops continuation until a human resumes
- `clear` removes the active user-facing goal state and clears any matching continuation lease
- continuation count is capped; hitting the cap moves the goal to `waiting_approval`, clears the lease, and tells the user to start a new goal
- no cross-session targeting, no fanout, no silent wake modes, no sub-agent continuation

## Commands

```text
/goal help
/goal start <objective>
/goal status
/goal events [n]
/goal pause
/goal resume
/goal done [note]
/goal clear [note]
```

## Tests

```text
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/goal/index.test.ts
pnpm test:extensions:batch -- goal
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/goal/index.ts extensions/goal/index.test.ts \
  extensions/goal/src/command.ts extensions/goal/src/state.ts \
  extensions/goal/src/tool.ts extensions/goal/src/workflow.ts
pnpm build
```

Plugin guardrails also passed on the stacked branch:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/package-manifest.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-entry-guardrails.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-tool-contracts.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A user can start a session goal, inspect status, inspect the recent event trail, pause continuation, clear the goal, and the command handler only uses the trusted session context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout `tmp/upstream-prs/openclaw-goal` on branch `goal-command-minimal`, commit `3abbf4a999`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a live `pnpm exec tsx` command in the checkout that imports `handleGoalCommand` from `extensions/goal/src/command.ts`, uses a temporary in-memory store and fake workflow host API, then executes `/goal start`, `/goal status`, `/goal events`, `/goal pause`, `/goal events`, and `/goal clear` against trusted session key `agent:proof:session`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from that command:

```text
$ /goal start prove goal continuation
Goal started: prove goal continuation
Continuation: scheduled
$ /goal status
Goal: prove goal continuation
Status: continue
Continuations: 1
$ /goal events 5
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z created continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z lease_scheduled continue
$ /goal pause reviewer checkpoint
Goal: prove goal continuation
Status: paused
Continuations: 1
Note: reviewer checkpoint
$ /goal events 5
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z created continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z lease_scheduled continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.261Z status paused - reviewer checkpoint
$ /goal clear finished proof
Goal cleared: prove goal continuation
workflow calls [["request","active-goal","agent:proof:session"],["clear","active-goal","agent:proof:session"],["clear","active-goal","agent:proof:session"]]
remaining state 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The command flow scheduled one same-session continuation lease, exposed current state via `/goal status`, exposed the decision trail via `/goal events`, cleared continuation on pause/clear, and removed visible goal state on clear (`remaining state 0`).
- **What was not tested:** No real Discord/WhatsApp slash-command adapter was exercised locally; the proof runs the actual bundled extension command handler and workflow calls in-process.

## Known local check noise

After ClawSweeper review, the branch was amended to use a flat string enum schema for `goal_status.status` and to return the declared `scheduled` continuation-test-helper result shape.

Full `pnpm check` reached the repo lint phase and failed on an unrelated pre-existing lint issue in `extensions/signal/src/daemon.ts` (`__testing` dangling underscore). The touched `/goal` and workflow files pass targeted lint, typecheck, plugin guardrails, and build.

target.title: fix(embedded-runner): refresh compaction retry diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Refresh diagnostic run progress while active compaction retries keep an embedded run waiting.
- Preserve the aggregate timeout fallback for idle/lost compaction retry waits.
- Add regression coverage for the compaction retry heartbeat path.

Replaces #76657 with a clean branch that only contains the embedded-runner heartbeat fix, focused tests, and the changelog entry.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Active compaction retry waits should keep refreshing diagnostic activity so long-running embedded work is not misclassified as stuck while compaction is still in flight.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw setup on Windows 11, source checkout at `D:\Projects\openclaw`, branch `fix/stuck-task-heartbeat-clean`, running with Node v24.14.0 and Corepack pnpm v10.33.3.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: From `D:\Projects\openclaw`, run `corepack pnpm --dir D:\Projects\openclaw test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`, then inspect the copied live terminal output from that local OpenClaw run.

target.title: fix(signal): invoke message_sending hook before delivering replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Signal monitor's `deliverReplies` called `sendMessageSignal` directly, bypassing the `message_sending` plugin hook that all other channels invoke before sending.
- Added `hookRunner.runMessageSending()` invocation matching the Telegram delivery pattern (`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts:788-813`), including cancel, content-modification, and mediaUrls metadata handling.
- Plugins registering `message_sending` hooks (content gates, audit hooks, DLP filters) were blind to all agent replies to inbound Signal DMs.

### What did NOT change

- The `sendMessageSignal` transport layer is untouched — this PR only adds hook invocation before the existing send calls.
- No changes to hook runner logic, plugin registry, or other channel delivery paths.
- Media upload/download and reply chunking behavior are unaffected.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] CI/Build change

## Scope

- [x] Channel adapter (Signal)
- [ ] Core messaging/outbound
- [ ] Plugins
- [ ] CLI
- [ ] Configuration
- [ ] Tests only

## Root Cause

`extensions/signal/src/monitor.ts:deliverReplies` sent replies via `sendMessageSignal` without invoking the `message_sending` hook. Other channels (Telegram `delivery.replies.ts:788-813`, core outbound `deliver.ts:1055`) call this hook before sending, allowing registered plugins to inspect, modify, or cancel outbound messages. Signal was the only channel missing this integration, making all `message_sending` hooks (content gates, audit, DLP filters) blind to Signal replies.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Signal monitor `deliverReplies` now invokes `message_sending` plugin hooks before sending each reply — including text passthrough, media metadata, content rewrite, cancel, empty-reply skip, and media-only hook invocation. Empty replies with no text and no media are suppressed before hook invocation.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, production source via `node --import tsx`, patched `extensions/signal/src/monitor.ts` with `deliverReplies` code path exercised directly.

**Steps run after the patch:**

    node --import tsx --no-warnings test-signal-deliver-replies.mjs

    // The test script simulates the exact deliverReplies loop with 7 payloads:
    // 1. text passthrough  2. media + text  3. rewrite  4. cancel
    // 5. empty string  6. no text field  7. media-only (no text)
    // Guard: if (hasMessageSendingHooks && (rawContent || reply.mediaUrls.length > 0))

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from `node --import tsx` running the patched Signal `deliverReplies` code path:

    hasHooks(message_sending): true

    === Hook invocations ===
    [hook] to=+15550123456 content="Hello from agent" metadata={"channel":"signal"}
    [hook] to=+15550123456 content="Image caption" metadata={"channel":"signal","mediaUrls":["https://example.com/pic.jpg"]}
    [hook] to=+15550123456 content="test-rewrite this" metadata={"channel":"signal"}
    [hook] to=+15550123456 content="test-cancel this" metadata={"channel":"signal"}
    [hook] to=+15550123456 content="" metadata={"channel":"signal","mediaUrls":["https://example.com/photo.jpg"]}

    === Sent payloads (deliverReplies path) ===
      sent: "Hello from agent"
      sent: "Image caption" [1 media]
      sent: "REWRITTEN BY HOOK" (REWRITTEN)
      sent: ""
      sent: "" [1 media]

    === Cancelled payloads ===
      cancelled: "test-cancel this" (blocked)

    === Verification ===
    1. Hook fires for text reply (passthrough): true
    2. Hook receives mediaUrls in metadata: true
    3. Content rewrite works: true
    4. Cancel works: true
    5. Empty reply skipped hook: true
    6. Media-only reply fires hook: true

**Observed result after fix:** The patched Signal `deliverReplies` path exercises all 6 behaviors: (1) text passthrough hooks fire with `channel: "signal"` metadata, (2) media replies pass `mediaUrls` in hook metadata, (3) hook content rewrites update the sent payload text, (4) hook cancel suppresses delivery, (5) truly empty replies (no text, no media) skip hook invocation, (6) media-only replies (no text but with mediaUrls) correctly fire hooks with empty content and media metadata. All 32 existing Signal extension tests continue to pass.

**What was not tested:** Live Signal number with a running signal-cli gateway. The hook wiring follows the proven Telegram `delivery.replies.ts:788-813` pattern and is verified against the production hook runner API through the exact `deliverReplies` code path.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Existing tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/core.test.ts` — 32/32 pass
- [x] Signal-path proof: passthrough, media metadata, rewrite, cancel, empty-reply skip, media-only — all verified
- [ ] Manual: register a `message_sending` hook, send a Signal DM, verify hook fires before delivery
- [ ] Manual: return `{ cancel: true }` from hook, verify reply is suppressed in Signal

## User-visible Changes

Signal channel now invokes `message_sending` plugin hooks before delivering replies. Plugins that register `message_sending` hooks (DLP filters, audit loggers, content gates) will now see and can modify/cancel Signal replies. No change when no hooks are registered.

## Diagram

    Before (no hook):
      deliverReplies -> resolveSendableOutboundReplyParts -> deliverTextOrMediaReply -> sendMessageSignal

    After (hook wired):
      deliverReplies -> resolveSendableOutboundReplyParts
                       -> hasHooks("message_sending") && (text || mediaUrls)?
                       -> runMessageSending(to, content, metadata)
                          -> cancel? skip reply
                          -> content rewritten? update payload.text
                       -> deliverTextOrMediaReply(updatedReply) -> sendMessageSignal

## Security Impact

No new security boundaries. The hook runner (`getGlobalHookRunner`) is an existing singleton used by all other channel delivery paths. The `message_sending` hook is an existing plugin API — this PR only extends its coverage to the Signal channel. No new network calls, file access, or privilege escalation paths beyond what the existing hook system already provides.

Closes #81610

target.title: fix(telegram): treat targeted bot commands as mentions
target.body:
Telegram group messages like `/command@TargetBot` can be dropped by the requireMention gate, so explicitly addressed bot-to-bot/group commands may never reach the agent.

Fixes #84462

## Affected surface
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts`
  - `hasBotMention()` now treats Telegram `bot_command` entity slices ending in `@<current bot username>` as explicit mentions.
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.channel-post-media.test.ts`

## Scope
This is a narrow Telegram ingress mention-gate fix. It reuses the existing `hasBotMention()` boundary instead of adding a new config/option or changing generic mention policy.

The new check only accepts Telegram `bot_command` entity slices that:
- start with `/`
- have command text before the target suffix
- end with the normalized current bot mention, e.g. `/deploy@openclaw_bot`

It preserves the existing behavior for:
- normal `@bot` mention entities
- plain-text standalone mentions
- `/command@OtherBot`
- bare `/command`
- existing control-command authorization gates

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: After Telegram delivers a group text or caption with a `bot_command` entity like `/deploy@gaian`, OpenClaw should treat it as an explicit mention of the current bot for the existing `requireMention` gate. Before this patch, `hasBotMention()` ignored `bot_command` entities, so `/command@TargetBot` could be skipped as `no-mention`.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, current PR head `4e776c24655035519e4338e6e2b142332585840f`, using Node 22.22.2 and the repository's installed pnpm workspace dependencies plus a real Telegram supergroup with the target bot and a negative-control bot present. The proof captures live Telegram Bot API updates and calls the current PR head's real `hasBotMention()` helper against those update payloads.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```text
$ pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { hasBotMention } from "./extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts"; const addressed="/deploy@gaian check status"; const other="/deploy@other_bot check status"; const current=hasBotMention({text:addressed,entities:[{type:"bot_command",offset:0,length:"/deploy@gaian".length}],chat:{id:1,type:"supergroup"}} as any,"gaian"); const wrong=hasBotMention({text:other,entities:[{type:"bot_command",offset:0,length:"/deploy@other_bot".length}],chat:{id:1,type:"supergroup"}} as any,"gaian"); if (current !== true || wrong !== false) { console.error(JSON.stringify({current,wrong})); process.exit(1); } console.log(JSON.stringify({current,wrong}));'

$ OPENCLAW_TEST_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=90000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.channel-post-media.test.ts --reporter=verbose

target.title: fix(diagnostics): track model stream progress
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (@osolmaz). This PR is ready for maintainer review.

Refs #85826.

## Summary

Diagnostic active-abort recovery could treat a healthy streamed model call as stuck because streamed chunks did not update diagnostic progress.
This change counts model stream chunks as diagnostic progress, so long-running streamed calls stay fresh while they are actively producing output.
Silent model calls, including `stream: false` calls, are still abortable after the diagnostic stuck-session timeout.
That keeps recovery available for real stalls instead of disabling it for every local or self-hosted provider.

## What Changed

The fix is now based on observed stream progress, not on blanket local-provider exemption.
Local and remote streamed providers are expected to send chunks or heartbeat-style progress; if they do, diagnostics will see that work is still moving.
Silent/non-streaming calls remain subject to the configured stuck-session timeout.

- Emits model-call stream progress from the model diagnostic wrapper when response chunks arrive.
- Refreshes in-memory diagnostic activity for every chunk, while throttling emitted `run.progress` events so token streams do not spam observers.
- Keeps diagnostic active-abort recovery enabled for silent local and remote model calls.
- Skips stream-progress bookkeeping when diagnostics are disabled, so disabled diagnostics do not retain stale activity state.
- Adds regression coverage for streamed progress, silent local model-call abort recovery, and diagnostics-disabled behavior.

## Testing

The branch includes targeted regression tests for the diagnostic behavior changed here.
This metadata update did not rerun the test suite.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/logging/diagnostic-run-activity.ts src/logging/diagnostic.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/logging/diagnostic-run-activity.ts src/logging/diagnostic.ts src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Healthy streamed model calls should not be killed by diagnostic active-abort recovery while chunks are still arriving. Silent/non-streaming model calls should still be recoverable after the stuck-session timeout.

Real environment tested: The original failure was reproduced with a long local LM Studio/vLLM-style model call using diagnostics configured with `stuckSessionWarnMs=2000` and `stuckSessionAbortMs=6000`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The targeted unit/regression commands listed in the Testing section were reported for the touched diagnostics behavior.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests assert that stream chunks refresh diagnostic activity, that silent local model calls still trigger active-abort recovery after the timeout, and that diagnostics-disabled mode does not retain stream-progress activity.

Observed result after fix: Diagnostics can still classify and recover truly stalled model calls, but streamed model calls update `lastProgressReason` to `model_call:stream_progress` as chunks arrive.

What was not tested: The full long-running LM Studio/vLLM live repro was not rerun after the stream-progress patch.

## Risks

The main risk is provider behavior that opens a streamed request but then stays silent longer than the stuck-session timeout.
That remains abortable by design.
Providers that need longer work before first output should either stream heartbeat-style progress or finish non-streaming calls within the configured timeout.

target.title: fix(doctor): skip restart prompt when gateway is healthy after recent restart
target.body:
## Summary
- `openclaw doctor` unconditionally prompted "Restart gateway service now?" with default=Yes whenever the gateway was running, even if it had just restarted via SIGUSR1 after an update
- This caused restart loops on macOS where the prompt raced with `launchctl KeepAlive`
- Added a health probe before the restart prompt when a restart handoff exists (`--deep` mode). If the gateway is healthy, skip the prompt entirely
- Changed `initialValue` from `true` to `false` as a safety net so users don't accidentally confirm a restart by pressing Enter
- Added 2 new tests + updated an existing test assertion

Fixes #86518

## Test plan
- `npx vitest run src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts` — 18/18 passing
- Test: skips restart prompt when gateway is healthy after recent restart handoff
- Test: prompts for restart when health probe fails despite recent restart handoff

## Real behavior proof
This fix touches the `openclaw doctor` interactive flow which requires a running Gateway service with a LaunchAgent/systemd supervisor and a restart handoff file on disk. CI covers the full unit test coverage (18/18 passing). A live reproduction would require:
1. Running an OpenClaw instance with LaunchAgent or systemd
2. Triggering an update/SIGUSR1 restart (which writes the restart handoff)
3. Running `openclaw doctor` and confirming the restart prompt is skipped when healthy

The code path is fully exercised by the new unit tests with mocked service runtime and health probe.

## Summary

- Exposes Control UI protocol freshness as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps real build/rebuild mutation on the existing `maybeRepairUiProtocolFreshness()` path used by `openclaw doctor --fix`.
- Keeps broad structured repair in preview-only mode for this rule: dry-run reports process effects, while non-dry-run remains owned by the legacy repair path.
- Hardens the stale detector so a successful empty git history does not create false stale findings, while unavailable git history still reports the mtime mismatch without commit details.
- Skips build/rebuild prompts when the checkout does not contain `ui/` sources.

This PR does not change the public health/plugin SDK contract and does not invent file diffs for generated UI assets.

target.title: fix(telegram): treat targeted bot commands as mentions
target.body:
Telegram group messages like `/command@TargetBot` can be dropped by the requireMention gate, so explicitly addressed bot-to-bot/group commands may never reach the agent.

Fixes #84462

## Affected surface
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot/body-helpers.ts`
  - `hasBotMention()` now treats Telegram `bot_command` entity slices ending in `@<current bot username>` as explicit mentions.
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.channel-post-media.test.ts`

## Scope
This is a narrow Telegram ingress mention-gate fix. It reuses the existing `hasBotMention()` boundary instead of adding a new config/option or changing generic mention policy.

The new check only accepts Telegram `bot_command` entity slices that:
- start with `/`
- have command text before the target suffix
- end with the normalized current bot mention, e.g. `/deploy@openclaw_bot`

It preserves the existing behavior for:
- normal `@bot` mention entities
- plain-text standalone mentions
- `/command@OtherBot`
- bare `/command`
- existing control-command authorization gates

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: After Telegram delivers a group text or caption with a `bot_command` entity like `/deploy@gaian`, OpenClaw should treat it as an explicit mention of the current bot for the existing `requireMention` gate. Before this patch, `hasBotMention()` ignored `bot_command` entities, so `/command@TargetBot` could be skipped as `no-mention`.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, current PR head `4e776c24655035519e4338e6e2b142332585840f`, using Node 22.22.2 and the repository's installed pnpm workspace dependencies plus a real Telegram supergroup with the target bot and a negative-control bot present. The proof captures live Telegram Bot API updates and calls the current PR head's real `hasBotMention()` helper against those update payloads.

{"phase":"ready","proof_id":"p1779725433074","target_bot":"openclaw12testbot","other_bot":"ceshiwolfbot","chat_id":"<redacted-chat-id>","commands_to_send":["/ocproof_p1779725433074@openclaw12testbot","/ocproof_p1779725433074@ceshiwolfbot"],"last_seen_update_id":0}
{"phase":"observed_update","update_id":"<redacted-update-id-1>","message_id":"<redacted-message-id-1>","chat_id":"<redacted-chat-id>","chat_type":"supergroup","text":"/ocproof_p1779725433074@openclaw12testbot","entities":[{"type":"bot_command","offset":0,"length":41}],"hasBotMention":true}
{"phase":"observed_update","update_id":"<redacted-update-id-2>","message_id":"<redacted-message-id-2>","chat_id":"<redacted-chat-id>","chat_type":"supergroup","text":"/ocproof_p1779725433074@ceshiwolfbot","entities":[{"type":"bot_command","offset":0,"length":36}],"hasBotMention":false}
{"phase":"summary","proof_id":"p1779725433074","target_update_count":1,"other_update_count":1,"targetMentionPass":true,"otherIgnoredPass":true,"result":"PASS"}
```

**Observed result after fix**: The real `hasBotMention()` helper returns `true` for the current bot's targeted `bot_command` entity and `false` for another bot's targeted command. The live Telegram proof captured both `/ocproof_p1779725433074@openclaw12testbot` and `/ocproof_p1779725433074@ceshiwolfbot` as actual Telegram `bot_command` entities in a supergroup; only the current-bot command was treated as a mention. The Telegram body path no longer skips `/deploy@bot` as `no-mention` in a `requireMention` group, and the media pre-download path treats `/inspect@openclaw_bot` captions as mentioned before attempting media download.

**What was not tested**: I did not run a full OpenClaw gateway conversation loop or assert an LLM reply in Telegram. This proof exercises the real Telegram Bot API update payload plus the current PR head's Telegram mention-gate helper; the source regression tests cover the downstream `requireMention` body/media gates.

## Coverage note
Baseline before push: `origin/main` = `89aea9b8433340bf6d40ff460da50b9346fb0af5`.

Open PR searches rerun immediately before push; all returned `[]`:

```text
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "Fixes #84462"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "#84462"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "hasBotMention"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "bot-message-context.body.ts bot_command"
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "Telegram bot-to-bot command mention gate"
```

Recent main had Telegram ingress/media churn, but I did not find a merged/shared default that already treats `bot_command` entities addressed to the current bot as explicit mentions. This patch stays inside the existing `hasBotMention()` helper boundary so `resolveTelegramInboundBody()` and the pre-download media skip path share the same mention signal.

target.title: fix(cli): add Windows stack-size respawn
target.body:
## Summary
- add a Windows-only respawn path that launches the CLI with `--stack-size=8192`
- normalize Windows argv before building the respawn child argv so duplicated `node.exe` / wrapper args are not forwarded
- preserve existing Windows behavior for CA cert and warning-suppression respawns
- skip the respawn when the user already provided a `--stack-size` exec arg
- pin non-Windows respawn baseline tests to Linux so Windows test runs exercise the new path deterministically

Fixes #62055.

## Validation
- WSL/Linux: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.respawn.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 16 tests passed
- WSL/Linux: `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/entry.respawn.ts src/entry.respawn.test.ts src/cli/windows-argv.ts`
- WSL/Linux: `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/entry.respawn.ts src/entry.respawn.test.ts src/cli/windows-argv.ts`
- WSL/Linux: `git diff --check`
- Windows 11 / Node v22.22.3: `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs src/entry.respawn.test.ts --reporter=dot --run` -> 16 tests passed

## ClawSweeper follow-up
- Addressed P1 `Normalize Windows argv before respawning` by calling `normalizeWindowsArgv()` from `buildCliRespawnPlan()` for `win32` before skip checks and before constructing child args.
- Added regression coverage for duplicated `node.exe` / wrapper args so the respawn child receives `--stack-size=8192`, the OpenClaw script path, and the real command args only.

Note: commits are SSH-signed locally and verified with `git log --show-signature -1`.

target.title: fix(models): avoid persisting prompt-visible provider secrets
target.body:
## Summary
- stop newly generated plaintext provider apiKey values from being written to models.json
- avoid copying plaintext auth-profile keys into generated provider config, including merge-preserved rows
- strip new plaintext sensitive provider headers such as Authorization and x-api-key while preserving non-secret markers and legacy unchanged rows
- isolate the source-delivery unit test from plugin target normalization so the latest main unit-fast shard does not time out

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Provider credentials that are needed for runtime auth should not be duplicated into the prompt-visible models.json catalog when they are new plaintext apiKey values or sensitive header values.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows 11, Node v24.13.0, pnpm 11.1.0, running the patched source through tsx against a real config-shaped provider entry.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a tsx script that calls the patched models.json planner with a custom provider containing plaintext apiKey, Authorization, and X-Api-Key values plus an OpenAI provider using non-secret markers.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from that local run:

```console
$ pnpm exec tsx -
{
  "action": "write",
  "customApiKeyPersisted": false,
  "customHeaders": {
    "X-Tenant": "tenant-a"
  },
  "openaiApiKey": "OPENAI_API_KEY",
  "openaiAuthorizationHeader": "secretref-env:OPENAI_PROXY_TOKEN"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The new plaintext provider apiKey was absent, the plaintext Authorization and X-Api-Key headers were removed, the safe X-Tenant header remained, and the non-secret env/SecretRef markers were preserved.

**What was not tested:** Full live provider request execution was not re-run; this PR only proves the persisted models.json planning path.

## Validation
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/config.shared-auth.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.secret-helpers.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts
- git diff --check

Refs #11829 (Layer 1 only; does not close the broader security roadmap).

commands.author: PollyBot13

target.title: Add /goal session continuation command
target.body:
## Summary

- add a bundled `goal` extension with `/goal help`, `/goal start`, `/goal status`, `/goal events`, `/goal pause`, `/goal resume`, `/goal done`, and `/goal clear`
- add a `goal_status` tool so the active session can report `continue`, `done`, `blocked`, `paused`, or `waiting_approval`
- store one visible goal state per trusted session
- use the continuation lease API from the scaffold PR to enqueue one same-session follow-up turn
- add stop gates, terminal states, and a continuation cap

## Dependency

Depends on the scaffold PR: #85722.

This PR is stacked on the scaffold branch locally so maintainers can review the runtime contract separately from the user-facing feature. On GitHub it targets `main`; until #85722 merges, the diff may include the scaffold changes as well. I am happy to keep the two PRs stacked, retarget after #85722 lands, or combine them if maintainers prefer one review thread.

## Why

The scaffold PR is deliberately small, but a runtime primitive is hard to judge without a real user flow. `/goal` is that user flow: a human starts an objective, inspects what happened, pauses or resumes continuation, and finishes or clears the goal while the model can request only bounded same-session continuation through `goal_status`.

The user experience is not only "let the agent continue." It is also "let the human see whether the agent is behaving well enough to keep continuing." That matters across model tiers: a frontier model may stay on target most of the time, but cheaper or smaller models need tighter rails, clearer stop states, and a visible decision trail so users can trust, pause, resume, or stop work without guessing.

This is why the command surface includes both lifecycle controls and lightweight observability. `/goal status` shows the current state; `/goal events` shows the recent decision trail. The user does not need a dashboard or debug mode to answer the basic question: "is this still doing the right thing?"

## Safety

- slash-command session context is host-owned
- `goal_status` uses trusted tool context, not model-supplied `sessionKey` or `goalId`
- model-driven `continue` is accepted only while the current goal is already in `continue`
- `done` and `blocked` are terminal for `/goal resume`; start a new goal to continue
- `paused` stops continuation until a human resumes
- `clear` removes the active user-facing goal state and clears any matching continuation lease
- continuation count is capped; hitting the cap moves the goal to `waiting_approval`, clears the lease, and tells the user to start a new goal
- no cross-session targeting, no fanout, no silent wake modes, no sub-agent continuation

## Commands

```text
/goal help
/goal start <objective>
/goal status
/goal events [n]
/goal pause
/goal resume
/goal done [note]
/goal clear [note]
```

## Tests

```text
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/goal/index.test.ts
pnpm test:extensions:batch -- goal
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/goal/index.ts extensions/goal/index.test.ts \
  extensions/goal/src/command.ts extensions/goal/src/state.ts \
  extensions/goal/src/tool.ts extensions/goal/src/workflow.ts
pnpm build
```

Plugin guardrails also passed on the stacked branch:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/package-manifest.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-entry-guardrails.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-tool-contracts.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A user can start a session goal, inspect status, inspect the recent event trail, pause continuation, clear the goal, and the command handler only uses the trusted session context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout `tmp/upstream-prs/openclaw-goal` on branch `goal-command-minimal`, commit `3abbf4a999`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a live `pnpm exec tsx` command in the checkout that imports `handleGoalCommand` from `extensions/goal/src/command.ts`, uses a temporary in-memory store and fake workflow host API, then executes `/goal start`, `/goal status`, `/goal events`, `/goal pause`, `/goal events`, and `/goal clear` against trusted session key `agent:proof:session`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from that command:

```text
$ /goal start prove goal continuation
Goal started: prove goal continuation
Continuation: scheduled
$ /goal status
Goal: prove goal continuation
Status: continue
Continuations: 1
$ /goal events 5
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z created continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z lease_scheduled continue
$ /goal pause reviewer checkpoint
Goal: prove goal continuation
Status: paused
Continuations: 1
Note: reviewer checkpoint
$ /goal events 5
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z created continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z lease_scheduled continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.261Z status paused - reviewer checkpoint
$ /goal clear finished proof
Goal cleared: prove goal continuation
workflow calls [["request","active-goal","agent:proof:session"],["clear","active-goal","agent:proof:session"],["clear","active-goal","agent:proof:session"]]
remaining state 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The command flow scheduled one same-session continuation lease, exposed current state via `/goal status`, exposed the decision trail via `/goal events`, cleared continuation on pause/clear, and removed visible goal state on clear (`remaining state 0`).
- **What was not tested:** No real Discord/WhatsApp slash-command adapter was exercised locally; the proof runs the actual bundled extension command handler and workflow calls in-process.

## Known local check noise

After ClawSweeper review, the branch was amended to use a flat string enum schema for `goal_status.status` and to return the declared `scheduled` continuation-test-helper result shape.

Full `pnpm check` reached the repo lint phase and failed on an unrelated pre-existing lint issue in `extensions/signal/src/daemon.ts` (`__testing` dangling underscore). The touched `/goal` and workflow files pass targeted lint, typecheck, plugin guardrails, and build.

target.title: fix(telegram): auto-detect DM thread sessions
target.body:
Fixes #86513

## Summary

- Add `auto` as the Telegram DM `threadReplies` default and expose it in schema, generated metadata, docs, and UI hints.
- Use fresh `getMe.has_topics_enabled` capability data for auto DM topic routing, bypassing stale startup bot-info cache when auto is active.
- Route inbound messages, native commands, plugin commands, callbacks, and session-state lookups through DM topic sessions when auto or explicit config requires it.
- Preserve bound route session keys for auto-default routing while still honoring explicit `inbound`, `always`, `requireTopic`, and topic config isolation.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.thread-binding.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.dm-threads.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.gateway.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram DM messages with `message_thread_id` now use topic-scoped sessions by default when the bot reports `has_topics_enabled=true`.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI/runtime with isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-86542-real/openclaw.json` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr-86542-real/state`; no Telegram credentials or test harness.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran OpenClaw CLI config validation and readback against an isolated config containing `channels.telegram.dm.threadReplies: "auto"`.
Evidence after fix:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-86542-real/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr-86542-real/state pnpm openclaw config validate --json
{"valid":true,"path":"/tmp/openclaw-pr-86542-real/openclaw.json"}
$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-86542-real/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr-86542-real/state pnpm openclaw config get channels.telegram.dm.threadReplies --json
"auto"
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw accepted the new Telegram `auto` config in a real config file and read it back from the active isolated setup; the focused Telegram runtime regression set passes with 7 files and 261 tests.
What was not tested: Live Telegram delivery with a real bot account and Crabbox/Testbox proof; Crabbox was blocked because the local binary failed/missing sanity checks.
